name: Meropenem
indications:
## Main indications

Spectrum similar to imipenem.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Powder vial containing 1 g (Meropenem Drawer, Fabra, FADA, Klonal, Larjan, Richet, Anfietoc).
adulto.reconstitution: 10 mL sterile water for injection. Conc: 50 mg/mL.
adulto.diluent: 500 mg in 100 mL NS or 5% dextrose.
adulto.administration: Direct IV: Yes. Shake until dissolved in 10 mL SWFI, administer over 5 min. Intermittent IV: Yes. Dilute in 100 mL NS or 5% dextrose and infuse over 30-60 min.
adulto.notes: With fluid restriction, may be diluted in as little as 50 mL NS or 5% dextrose.

pediatrico.reconstitution: Sterile water for injection for reconstitution.
pediatrico.dose: Sepsis, CNS infections and cystic fibrosis: 120 mg/kg/day every 8 h, maximum dose: 6 g/day. Urinary tract, skin and soft tissue and other infections: 60 mg/kg/day every 8 h, maximum dose: 3 g/day. Pulmonary, intestinal tuberculosis: 60 mg/kg/day every 8 h, maximum 1,000 mg/dose. Meningeal tuberculosis: 120 mg/kg/day every 8 h, maximum 2,000/dose. See tuberculosis treatment guide.
pediatrico.notes: Lower CNS toxicity than imipenem. Push administration: 3 to 5 min; intermittent infusion: 15 to 30 min.

neonatal.dose: Dose and interval per postmenstrual age and weight (e.g. 20 mg/kg/dose every 12–24 h in preterm infants; medical adjustment).

stability:
## Reconstituted (in vial)

- 1 h at room temperature, 8 h refrigerated.

## Diluted solution (for administration)

- In NS: 8 h at room temperature, 48 h refrigerated. In 5% dextrose: 3 h at room temperature, 14 h refrigerated.

## Pediatric guide

- 2 h at room temperature. 12 h refrigerated.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Headache, nausea, abdominal pain, diarrhea.
